name: Fullstack AI App Stack
thesis: Ship a production AI app in a weekend with auth, payments, streaming, and a database.

stack.role: Next.js 16 + Tailwind + shadcn/ui + Prisma scaffold
Production-grade React component library (notchatbot UI, sidebars, dashboards)
Design-system rules: spacing, typography scale, color tokens
GSAP animation patterns for React (ScrollTrigger, Flip, stagger)
Streaming chat completions, multi-turn context

why: Covers the full surface area of an AI SaaS: UI (shadcn + 21st), motion (GSAP), design discipline (ui-ux-pro-max), and the AI itself (LLM SDK). Each skill fills a real gap the others leave.
use: Vibes-coded MVPs, hackathon projects, internal AI tools, paid weekend builds.
health: All skills are TypeScript-native and don't conflict. The only health cost is bundle size — code-split GSAP and Three.js aggressively.

name: Immersive Landing Page Stack
thesis: Build a launch-page that converts through motion depth, not copy.

stack.role: WebGL scenes, shaders, particle systems
React Three Fiber patterns + shader material recipes
ScrollTrigger, pinning, scrub
Typography hierarchy, color discipline so the 3D doesn't dominate

why: Three.js + GSAP is the standard immersive combo. ui-ux-pro-max prevents the common failure mode where 3D swamps the content. Two Three.js skills cover both vanilla (claudekit) and React (freshtechbro) patterns.
use: Product launches, agency portfolios, film/game marketing, NFT drops.
health: High bundle cost (Three.js ~600kb, GSAP ~50kb). Must lazy-load below-the-fold. Accessibility: always provide a static fallback for prefers-reduced-motion. SEO: render critical content server-side.

name: Content Engine Stack
thesis: Generate, schedule, and analyze content at scale across blog, social, and email.

stack.role: Multi-turn article generation, tone control, summarization
Live research for fact-grounded content
Source extraction, citation gathering
Transcribe voice memos into draft posts
Hero illustrations, social cards

why: End-to-end content pipeline: search (research) → reader (extract) → LLM (draft) → image-gen (visuals) → TTS/ASR (repurpose to/from audio). All built into z-ai-web-dev-sdk, so zero external dependencies.
use: Solo creator ops, agency content teams, SEO programs, podcast repurposing.
health: All skills share one SDK — zero dependency conflicts. Main cost is API tokens; cache aggressively and dedupe sources. Quality gate: human edit pass before publish.

name: MCP Tool → Monetize Stack
thesis: Build a paid MCP server that Claude Desktop users subscribe to.

stack.role: Landing page + dashboard for the SaaS wrapper
In-tool reasoning, prompt routing
API routes + Prisma for credit tracking + Stripe webhooks
Live data the MCP tool can call

why: MCP tools need (a) the tool itself (TypeScript MCP SDK, not a skill), (b) a monetization wrapper (Stripe + Prisma + Next.js = fullstack-dev), (c) a marketing surface (21st-dev-components), and (d) often a live-data source (web-search).
use: Vertical MCP tools for specific roles (recruiters, lawyers, SDRs).
health: Stripe webhooks must be idempotent. Use Prisma's unique constraints + a webhook_log table. Always handle customer.subscription.deleted to revoke credits.

name: Deep Research Stack
thesis: Run a multi-source, citation-grounded research pass on any question.

stack.role: Discover sources
Extract clean text from each source
Synthesize, with explicit citation instructions
Export structured findings to a spreadsheet deliverable
Generate the final research report as a polished PDF

why: Mirrors the gpt-researcher pattern: search → read → synthesize → deliver. Each skill maps to one stage. Output is a real artifact (PDF + XLSX), not chat text.
use: Consulting deliverables, due diligence, market research, competitive intel.
health: LLM synthesis can hallucinate citations. Force the LLM to output [source N] inline tags and verify each against the reader output. Reject any unverified claim.

name: Design System Stack
thesis: Codify a brand design system into reusable tokens, components, and lint rules.

stack.role: Token hierarchy, spacing scale, typography system
Production component primitives
Token pipeline (Tailwind 4 @theme), Storybook-free docs
Chart-color system, dashboard patterns

why: A design system isn't just components — it's tokens + components + chart patterns + documentation. The four skills cover all four layers. ui-ux-pro-max enforces discipline that 21st-dev-components alone won't.
use: Startups formalizing their UI, agencies handing off to client teams, open-source design systems.
health: Token naming collisions between ui-ux-pro-max and shadcn defaults. Adopt a prefix convention (--brand- vs --color-) and document it.
